citations_found.summary: Legal Document View ] \ , Cited by \] Patna High Court Balaji Infraro Ltd vs Chanakya National Law University & Ors on 16 November, 2016 Author: Bench: ``` IN THE HIGH COURT OF JUDICATURE AT PATNA Request Case No.8 of 2014 =========================================================== Balaji Infraro Ltd. a company registered under the Company Act, 1956 having its registered office at CJ-276, Sector-II, Salt Lake City, Kolkata-700091, through its Director, Soumyapur Sen Gupta son of Mr. Subhash Sen ...
citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute was between Balaji Infraro (I) Ltd and Chanakya National Law University over a claim for extra carriage costs of Rs.63,18,345.49 incurred during the construction of a Boys Hostel. The court decided that the claim for extra carriage costs was not admissible as it was not specifically mentioned in the BOQ (Bill of Quantities) of civil work, and the petitioner-company had been informed of this earlier. The court thus upheld the decision of the consultant M/s Gherzi Eastern Ltd. that the claim was not admissible.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute in Kaushal Kishor vs The State Of Uttar Pradesh Govt. Of U.P. was about the interpretation of free speech and the limits of criticism towards public figures, particularly politicians. The court ultimately decided that while free speech is a fundamental right, it is not absolute and can be restricted if it causes harm or offense to others, particularly in the context of public discourse.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here's a 2-3 sentence summary of the case:

The dispute in this case was about the Maharashtra government's decision to impose a 1% tax on the interest income of banks and financial institutions, which was challenged by various banks and financial institutions, including Jalgaon Janta Sahakari Bank Ltd. and ICICI Bank Ltd. The Bombay High Court ultimately decided that the imposition of the tax was unconstitutional and ordered its repeal, citing the principles of federalism and the powers of the state government.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the case:

The dispute in Amway India Enterprises vs The Deputy Commissioner Of Income-Tax was about whether the expenditure incurred by the company on computer software was of revenue nature or capital nature, and if it was capital, what rate of depreciation would be applicable. The court ultimately decided that the expenditure on certain software, such as MS Project 2000 and Macromedia Dream weaver, was of capital nature, but the rate of depreciation was not specified in the order.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here's a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute in Rustam Phiroze Mehta vs State Of Maharashtra And Ors was about the petitioner's claim that the respondents had wrongfully demolished a portion of his property in Pune, Maharashtra, and the petitioner sought a writ of mandamus to restrain the respondents from demolishing the remaining portion. The court ultimately decided in favor of the petitioner, holding that the respondents had acted in excess of their jurisdiction and that the demolition of the property was unlawful. The court ordered the respondents to restore the demolished portion of the property to its original condition.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute in Balaji Infraro (I) Ltd vs Chanakya National Law University & Ors was about the petitioner-company's claim for an additional amount of Rs.63,18,345.49 for the "carriage of materials" in the construction of a Boys Hostel. The court decided that the claim for extra carriage was not admissible as it was not mentioned in the BOQ (Bill of Quantities) of the civil work, and therefore, the petitioner-company was not entitled to the additional amount.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute in Kaushal Kishor vs The State Of Uttar Pradesh Govt. Of U.P. was about the interpretation of the right to free speech under Article 19(1)(a) of the Indian Constitution, specifically whether it includes the right to make derogatory or defamatory statements. The court ultimately decided that the right to free speech is not absolute and that it can be restricted if it causes harm to others, emphasizing the importance of "sweet speech" as per ancient Indian and international literature.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a summary of the case in 2-3 sentences:

The dispute was about the validity of the Maharashtra Sales Tax on Dealers (Amendment) Act, 2013, which imposed a tax on cooperative banks and other financial institutions. The court decided that the amendment was unconstitutional and ultra vires, as it was not passed by a two-thirds majority in the Legislative Assembly, as required by Article 194(3) of the Constitution of India.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute in Amway India Enterprises vs The Deputy Commissioner Of Income-Tax was about whether the expenditure incurred by the company on computer software was of revenue nature or capital nature, and if it was capital, what rate of depreciation should be applicable. The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal decided that the expenditure on software was of capital nature, and the rate of depreciation applicable would be 20% per annum. This decision was made in the context of the company's claim for a deduction on account of software expenditure incurred for its business operations.

Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute in Rustam Phiroze Mehta vs State Of Maharashtra And Ors was about a land acquisition case involving the petitioner, Rustam Phiroze Mehta, and the respondents, including the State of Maharashtra and Marvel Sigma Homes Private Limited. The court ultimately decided in favor of the petitioner, directing the respondents to pay compensation and also to allow the petitioner to retain possession of the land.
